data.body:
PER-CHILD PLAN-REVIEW APPLIED (3-pass v8 harness on each of the 10 children; proportionate scrutiny level=story -> container G3/G4 + task-only criteria dropped; agentic verify on). Raw: tmp/children_review.json.

OUTCOME: 0 BLOCK on every child (advisory posture); ~96 advisory total, but dominated by the SAME cross-cutting themes firing per-child (all overlays forced on, no top-N cap). Triaged into specific defects vs cross-cutting policy.

SPECIFIC DEFECTS (per-child fixes):
1. S3 afe6 [CRITICAL, VERIFIED] resolve_prompt NAME COLLISION: prompts.py:297 already defines resolve_prompt(reviewer,variables,langfuse)->compiled (the render step), and load_prompt + get_reviewer also exist. The new unified resolver must be a DISTINCT name (get_prompt(prompt_id)->Prompt) and S3 must state the fate of all four (get_reviewer removed/folded; existing resolve_prompt render-step retained/adapted).
2. S4 4b2f + S3 [recurring critical] execution_mode DEFAULT/migration: absent execution_mode = agentic (back-compat); S3 migration stamps it on all migrated prompts.
3. S0 5e78 [real] lint must treat an upstream step with NO output contract as UNKNOWN/skip (never error) during incremental rollout; pin the ${{ inputs.* }} engine-var source-of-truth as a named constant.
4. S1 e050 [conf 1.0] What/AC mismatch: What says author INPUT schemas, AC2 requires OUTPUT-schema match + per-op output-shape test -> align (author+register both).
5. S8 a83a [real] specify how a structured field's setValue writes back into rebar:Config; define 'common path'; resolve body('replace') vs AC('common path') coherence.
6. S7 998e [real] define /validate response schema (errors[] path/message); state Save-blocked-on-error / unavailable policy.
7. S6 6592 [real] pin the palette category vocabulary + the bpmn-js insertion mechanism.
8. S2 d25d [recurring critical] schema_version WRITE-side deploy ordering (bump only when emitting a new key; upgrade readers before writers; read-side refuse is the net).

CROSS-CUTTING -> handle ONCE (not 10x churn):
- Docs/ADR (T7 every child): create ONE docs/ADR child story covering all the new conventions.
- Accessibility (T5d every UI child): epic decision = internal dev tool, a11y done-definition explicitly DEFERRED (documented, not silent).
- Rollback/feature-flag (T12/T4 recurring): epic note = internal git-versioned single-deployment tool; rollback = git revert (+ existing config gates for runtime-path changes S4/S5); no per-ticket canary infra.
- Testing failure-paths (E5 recurring): blanket expectation = each child's tests include failure/invalid/empty-path cases.
- G5 slicing: walking skeleton S0 is intentionally cross-layer; S4/S5 multi-layer is cohesive -> keep.
